name: Will new siding make my home quieter, or do I need new windows too?
headline: Will new siding make my home quieter, or do I need new windows too?

mainEntity.name: Will new siding make my home quieter, or do I need new windows too?
mainEntity.text: Will new siding make my home quieter, or do I need new windows too?

acceptedAnswer.text: In the Bay Area’s mix of fog, sun, and salt air, **both quality siding and good windows** help, but if your windows are old and leaky, upgrading them along with siding usually gives a bigger comfort and noise improvement. New siding helps seal the building envelope when combined with housewrap, flashing, and insulation upgrades. This can reduce drafts and some outside noise, especially if your old siding is damaged or poorly installed. However, a lot of noise and air leakage comes through old single‑pane or poorly sealed windows and doors. If you leave them in place while replacing siding, you miss a big opportunity. When siding is off, it is the best time to: - Replace old windows with modern double‑pane or laminated glass units. - Properly flash and air‑seal around the frames. - Adjust trim and siding details for a tighter fit. Pitfalls: - Doing siding now and windows later, forcing people to cut into new work. - Assuming that thicker siding alone will block freeway or airport noise. If budget is tight, consider focusing on the noisiest or draftiest side of the house for window upgrades while you do siding there. Plan the rest in phases so work doesn’t need to be redone.
